answers.description:
0 : No PPE prescribed, no monitoring.

1 : PPE is prescribed but not worn or displayed. PPE exists, is kept in good condition, and is properly stored.

2 : PPE is prescribed and displayed, by workstation, made available and partially worn by employees (at least 80%). PPE is made available to visitors.

3 : PPE is worn by employees and visitors (at least 90%). It is managed by the team: sufficient quantities are available, and a replenishment method is defined.

4 : PPE is worn by all employees and visitors (100%). Requirements are reviewed regularly (at least once a year), in conjunction with risk assessments.
answers.question: Personal protective equipment
answers.action_plan: Provide sufficient quantities of PPES at all times for use of both employees and visitors

answers.description:
0 : No safety signage. No specific communication on the subject.

1 : There is a display of the number of accidents resulting in lost time. The corresponding objective is defined. HSE audits are conducted at least once a year by the QHSE department. Safety is a priority topic during the shift handover and handover rituals.

2 : The HSE objectives are defined and understood by management. Staff feel that safety is a priority for management. Every lost-time accident is analyzed and an action plan is developed within 48 hours. The workshop manager participates in the annual HSE audits.

3 : First aid procedures are recorded and posted. Once a month, the workshop manager's structured safety walkthrough is dedicated to safety. On a daily basis, key safety points are checked through these structured walkthroughs.

4 : Near misses are reported and addressed. First aid incidents are analyzed twice a year, resulting in an action plan. A safety review, led by the line manager, takes place quarterly and covers all safety aspects of the line.
answers.question: Animation, Communication, Safety Display
answers.action_plan: No action needed

answers.description:
0 : Few defined and known rules. Little formal marking of fire extinguishers and fire hose reels. No first response team formed.

1 :  Critical workstations have safety instructions. Most fire extinguishers and fire hose reels are clearly marked.

2 : With very few exceptions, all workstations have safety instructions in addition to PPE guidelines. Fire extinguishers and fire hose reels are located and considered to be in sufficient quantity. A risk assessment has been carried out for the entire area.

3 : Safety instructions are posted and staff are trained (based on risk assessment). There are sufficient trained intervention and rescue teams for the area. Recycling is carried out regularly.

4 : Safety improvements are regularly implemented at workstations. Revised safety rules are posted, known, and followed by everyone. Staff are trained accordingly.
answers.question: Risk management
answers.action_plan: Deepen risk assessments of any jobs on site to ensure all risks are covered by regular reviews by safety team

answers.description:
0 : The actors whose operators have heard about the subject, nothing more.

1 :  Most people know the meaning of "5S" as: "Order, set in order, cleanliness".

2 : They talk about order, tidiness and cleanliness, but also about standardization and respecting standards. They have all undergone 5S training.

3 : The 5S methodology is seen as a tool for improving working conditions, including ergonomics. Many refer to "5S" as a mindset.

4 : Everyone has undergone 5S training. Even the operators suggest improvements. The "5S" is a mindset and a mentality shared by all.
answers.question: 5S training and awareness
answers.action_plan: 1. Roll out 5s training for selected team 2.Select pilot areas and apportion to various 5s teams - eg centrifuge area,BMF area, Brewing office, Eng workshop-Q2. Q2 carryout stage 2 5s for 2 selected areas in departments- eg packaging line 2 and engineering stores

answers.description:
0 : The 4th and 5th S programs need to be implemented for certain areas.

1 :  The 5S project exists, but is rarely a priority. The cleaning schedule exists and is more or less followed, even if informally.

2 : 3S Audit Score of 80%. The areas (Work + in progress) are systematically cleaned at the end of each shift by the operators.

3 : Work areas are cleaned in "real time". Supply and replenishment activities are synchronized with production operations.

4 : The audit score (3S) is consistently above 90%. The audit requirements are continuously improving. A refresher course is conducted annually.
answers.question: Mastery of the first 3 "S"s
answers.action_plan: Reimplement 5s training for selected team and trigger 5s on pilot basis in Q2 in all departments eg centrifuge area, BMF area, Brewing office, eng workshop and Pkg Line 2

answers.description:
0 : There are no rules, and therefore no respect for them.

1 : Rules exist but are only respected when the hierarchy intervenes. The flow is not clearly identified.

2 :  The rules are known, accepted, and respected. They concern raw materials, work in progress, waste,

3 : Audits are conducted regularly. 5S audit scores are above 80%. Most areas are identified. The workflow is quite clear.

4 : Improvements are proposed to optimize the work environment; operators are the driving force.
answers.question: Mastery of the last two "S"s
answers.action_plan: Select pilot areas and apportion to the 5s teams eg centrifuge area, BMF area, Brewing office, Eng workshop etc. for pkg pilot one line and roll out learnings to the other lines

answers.description:
0 : There are no information meetings within the teams, or else: team members are repeatedly absent or late. Meetings are held on an ad hoc basis; they are not routine.

1 :  Weekly information meetings are used to discuss major Unit topics. The ritual is not always respected: Location, duration, time, etc.

2 :  The weekly information meeting is a ritual in front of the notice board; it is used to discuss the performance of the past week and possible improvements.

3 :  The weekly meeting is held without any absences or lateness. The agenda is followed, and issues are not addressed during the meeting (appointments are scheduled). The format is strictly adhered to."12345". Representatives from support services may be invited occasionally.

4 : The meeting is prepared through interviews with the facilitator's direct supervisor and related production functions (planning, quality, methods, etc.). A structured site visit precedes the meeting to gather relevant observations. The resulting data is then analyzed.
answers.question: Weekly meeting
answers.action_plan: Monitor rigor of meeting by evaluations of weekly meetings and feedback to teams at tier 1 and tier 2 especially.

answers.description:
0 : Daily rituals are not formal. If rules exist, they are often not followed. The companions are left to their own devices at the start of their shift.

1 : Progress and key points (safety, quality, and maintenance) are recorded. Informal oral exchanges take place between direct supervisors and team members or colleagues. The area manager assigns everyone to their workstation. The established procedures have been described.

2 : The rituals take place. The agenda is generally followed. Absences and lateness are noted. The facilitators use a supporting document (paper) and record the main points on a daily chart.

3 : The discussion of the previous day's problems leads to corrective actions. The facilitator's direct supervisor sometimes attends the meeting.

4 : The routines—shift start, shift end, and shift handover—are perfectly followed in all areas. No delays, no time overruns, and adherence to the agenda. Corrective actions are implemented and monitored.
answers.question: Daily rituals of frontline management.
answers.action_plan: Monitor daily rituals at tier 1 by regular validation SHO, SICs, safety , RCPS triggers, Board updates etc. These are inputs to daily rituals

answers.description:
0 :  The board doesn't operate regularly. It's present on the ground, but not in a structured way and without a specific purpose.

1 : Management rotates regularly, but without any structured process. They use their observations immediately after each rotation, in an informal manner.

2 : The tours are ritualized and can be thematic. A formalized assessment tool for the standards to be monitored is in place. It includes quality, safety, 5S, and adherence to rules.

3 : The progress of the practices monitored during field visits is tracked over time. Field visits are known to and valued by operators as a way to gather feedback.

4 : Any deviation from accepted standards is addressed through an action plan. Middle management participates regularly in the review. Senior management participates occasionally.
answers.question: Structured field tour by the shift leader.
answers.action_plan: Ensure adherence to keeping records of structured walkabout observations by valiadions by HODs

answers.description:
0 : No display or outdated display or notice boards exist and everyone is free to display the information they deem relevant.

1 :  Designated areas are provided for displaying zone information. A list of information/indicators to be displayed has been defined. The display is implemented and kept up to date.

2 : dedicated space is provided for displaying objectives. A list of information/indicators to be displayed has been defined. The display is implemented and kept up to date. The operators have a good understanding of the zone indicators.

3 :  The operators have a good understanding of the line indicators. The overall presentation of the display boards complies with the 5S principles.

4 :  The content of the table display is reviewed every two years (involving team members) to remove unnecessary information and add relevant information.
answers.question: Display
answers.action_plan: Agree on contents of boards and refresh them to agree with new manex roll out

answers.description:
0 : There are no on-the-ground indicators. There is no data collection and no performance target.

1 :  Indicators exist for the zones and are tailored to the target audiences (relevant for the manager and understandable by the operators). They are updated regularly. All operators are aware of their contribution.

2 : Objectives exist for each zone; they are realistic and ambitious. Zone indicators are discussed during the corresponding rituals. Indicators also exist for the line; they are tailored to the audience and regularly updated.

3 :  Objectives exist for each line of work; they are realistic and ambitious. Line indicators are discussed during the corresponding rituals. Each deviation from the objective elicits a reaction from management (why?).

4 : Team members suggest new indicators and propose action plans to respond to them. Objectives are regularly met and raised.
answers.question: Terrain Indicators & Walking Target
answers.action_plan: Routinise shift handovers to action deviations in zone and line indicators

answers.description:
0 : No self-control

1 : Self-monitoring is defined and implemented on the most critical production workstations. However, reliability is not verified.

2 : Self-inspection is in place at all workstations and the results are recorded. Operators have been trained and know the procedure to follow in case of a defect. Standards are posted at the workstation. Records are sometimes incomplete.

3 : Self-monitoring is monitored and audited by management; records are complete. A system for reporting defects detected at the customer's site exists and allows for monitoring effectiveness.

4 : Operators make proposals to improve quality. Standards are reviewed taking into account customer feedback.
answers.question: Self-control
answers.action_plan: Ensure all defect information gets fed back to site to elicit corrective action

answers.description:
0 : No detection of non-conformities in production or no monitoring

1 :  Non-conforming product types are identified. Non-conforming products are detected, isolated, and processed using informal procedures. They are then reported.

2 : Formalized procedures exist for handling non-conformities. The types of non-conformities are recorded and classified (ABC diagram), the results are displayed, and the costs of the non-conformities are calculated.

3 : The main non-conformities are addressed through problem-solving groups, and action plans are implemented. Quality indices and customer feedback (PHN) are displayed and communicated to staff.

4 : 80% of non-conformities are addressed through problem-solving groups. A positive trend in quality assurance results and customer feedback has been observed following the actions taken.
answers.question: Mastering NCs in production
answers.action_plan: Cost of NC products info should be trickled down to the shop floor to help operatives understand the cost of their actions or inactions

answers.description:
0 : The teams are not aware of the concept of OEE and are not conscious of the losses.

1 :  The OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) is measured systematically and the results are displayed.

2 : Improvement targets are set. The main losses are systematically identified (Pareto analysis). Actions to eliminate losses are carried out periodically.

3 : Improvement rituals take place every week. They are prepared, a report is written, and an action plan is developed. The action plan is reviewed weekly, posted, and communicated to the operators. Participants are not trained in problem-solving.

4 : Problem-solving tools are used systematically, and several members are trained in problem-solving. Operators and mechanics participate in these meetings. action_plan deadlines are met, results are improving, and objectives are achieved.
answers.question: Handling yield losses

answers.description:
0 :  The teams are not aware of the concept of shrinkage and are not conscious of the losses

1 : The shrinkage is measured systematically and the results are displayed.

3 : Improvement rituals take place every week. They are prepared, a report is written, and an action plan is developed. The action plan is reviewed weekly, posted, and communicated to the operators. Participants are not trained in problem-solving.

4 : Problem-solving tools are used systematically, and several members are trained in problem-solving. Operators and mechanics participate in these meetings. action_plan deadlines are met, results are improving, and objectives are achieved.
answers.question: Treatment of shrinkages

answers.description:

1 : Standard worksheets exist, are designed by the various departments (methods, maintenance, etc.), but are rarely, if ever, used.

4 : Standards are the foundation of continuous improvement. Deviations from the standard worksheet are systematically treated as problems to be solved. A history of the problems addressed is kept.
answers.question: Working with standards

answers.description:

2 : A pilot project on one machine was carried out. Staff were trained and participated. The standard is displayed.

3 :  Approximately 50% of the machines are operating under SMED. Implementation plan established.

4 :  All equipment is manufactured using SMED (Single Minute Exchange of Die). This process is repeated on at least one machine each year to continuously improve performance.
answers.question: SMED (CIP between brews/filtration cycles)

answers.description:

4 :  Improvements are made to cleaning, lubrication and inspection standards every year
answers.question: Autonomous maintenance
answers.action_plan: Retrain operators in AM in all departments and carryout on pilot basis eg MF, BMF & CIP plants in brewing. For packaging select eg labeller in addition to filler 2

answers.description:

answers.question: Detection and reporting of anomalies

answers.description:

answers.question: Preventive maintenance
answers.action_plan: Some machines break down just after PMs. Review quality of PMs using data based decision making. Improve ordering system to ensure parts are available during PMs

answers.description:

4 : 80% of the stakeholders were trained in problem-solving. Improvements in results have been observed. Root cause analysis leads to the implementation of standards.

answers.description:
0 : The line managers (area supervisors, foremen, and line managers) are running around without peace of mind. Their tasks are not clearly defined or structured. Respecting time and schedules is not a priority.

1 : Line managers review their activity and discuss it with their supervisors, who provide informal support. The employees have not received formal training in time management, although some may have been made aware of the concept.

2 : The line managers have a standard schedule. Any deviation from the schedule is considered a problem to be addressed. The tasks on the standard schedule answer the question, "What is important?" The managers have received time management training.

3 : Delegation and skills development for team members is seen as a win-win solution (related to behavioral aspects). All managers have received time management training. The standard schedule is starting to be followed.

4 : The standard agenda is maintained, improved and displayed; it includes time slots dedicated to structured problem-solving.
answers.question: Time management
answers.action_plan: Additional training in good to great coaching and other management tools to support managers to be more effective

answers.description:
0 : No visible rules of conduct. No respect for basic rules: mutual listening, making suggestions, sharing ideas, etc. The managers have not received any management training.

1 : Some teamwork practices are observed. The managers have some basic, but not academic, management skills. Difficulties are noted among the managers in managing their teams.

2 : The line managers have undergone management training. They act as coaches for their team members. They succeed in developing responsiveness and proactivity in their team members.

3 : Frontline managers gain the team's buy-in and motivation. The team is able to define and implement common rules.

4 :  The team functions perfectly by applying the rules it has defined. Team cohesion is evident.
answers.question: Behavior and role of frontline management.
answers.action_plan: Train all in Rcps.implement good to great coaching

answers.description:
0 : Versatility is not managed. Generally, each person sticks to their assigned role.

1 : The skills of the operators at their workstations are known but not formalized. And the use of the tool is limited to managing leave and absences.

2 : The tool (matrix P) exists, is formatted, and is displayed. The update frequency is determined and adhered to. The tool is used in the daily management of team work. Staff rotations are planned and followed.

3 : Management uses the tool to develop its teams by creating the annual training plan. The same principle is used to manage the positions of foreman and area manager.

4 : The team has taken ownership of the tool: it is constantly being updated. They provide feedback to management regarding training needs and suggestions for organization, etc. The training plan takes into account the needs expressed by staff and discussed with management.
answers.question: Versatility
answers.action_plan: Develop a formal skills matrix for brewing and develop action plans from it

answers.description:
0 : No PPE prescribed, no monitoring.

1 : PPE is prescribed but not worn or displayed. PPE exists, is kept in good condition, and is properly stored.

2 : PPE is prescribed and displayed, by workstation, made available and partially worn by employees (at least 80%). PPE is made available to visitors.

3 : PPE is worn by employees and visitors (at least 90%). It is managed by the team: sufficient quantities are available, and a replenishment method is defined.

4 : PPE is worn by all employees and visitors (100%). Requirements are reviewed regularly (at least once a year), in conjunction with risk assessments.
answers.question: Personal protective equipment
answers.action_plan: Reviewed in the daily Tier meeting. Request for non availability is done based on required personenel

answers.description:
0 : No safety signage. No specific communication on the subject.

1 : There is a display of the number of accidents resulting in lost time. The corresponding objective is defined. HSE audits are conducted at least once a year by the QHSE department. Safety is a priority topic during the shift handover and handover rituals.

2 : The HSE objectives are defined and understood by management. Staff feel that safety is a priority for management. Every lost-time accident is analyzed and an action plan is developed within 48 hours. The workshop manager participates in the annual HSE audits.

3 : First aid procedures are recorded and posted. Once a month, the workshop manager's structured safety walkthrough is dedicated to safety. On a daily basis, key safety points are checked through these structured walkthroughs.

4 : Near misses are reported and addressed. First aid incidents are analyzed twice a year, resulting in an action plan. A safety review, led by the line manager, takes place quarterly and covers all safety aspects of the line.
answers.question: Animation, Communication, Safety Display
answers.action_plan: Animation videos available and viewed on the screen in the main lab

answers.description:
0 : Few defined and known rules. Little formal marking of fire extinguishers and fire hose reels. No first response team formed.

1 :  Critical workstations have safety instructions. Most fire extinguishers and fire hose reels are clearly marked.

2 : With very few exceptions, all workstations have safety instructions in addition to PPE guidelines. Fire extinguishers and fire hose reels are located and considered to be in sufficient quantity. A risk assessment has been carried out for the entire area.

3 : Safety instructions are posted and staff are trained (based on risk assessment). There are sufficient trained intervention and rescue teams for the area. Recycling is carried out regularly.

4 : Safety improvements are regularly implemented at workstations. Revised safety rules are posted, known, and followed by everyone. Staff are trained accordingly.
answers.question: Risk management
answers.action_plan: Daily and weekly review of work place risk, during thr tier meetings

answers.description:
0 : The actors whose operators have heard about the subject, nothing more.

1 :  Most people know the meaning of "5S" as: "Order, set in order, cleanliness".

2 : They talk about order, tidiness and cleanliness, but also about standardization and respecting standards. They have all undergone 5S training.

3 : The 5S methodology is seen as a tool for improving working conditions, including ergonomics. Many refer to "5S" as a mindset.

4 : Everyone has undergone 5S training. Even the operators suggest improvements. The "5S" is a mindset and a mentality shared by all.
answers.question: 5S training and awareness
answers.action_plan: Weekly audit and reviewed in the weekly tier meeting

answers.description:
0 : The 4th and 5th S programs need to be implemented for certain areas.

1 :  The 5S project exists, but is rarely a priority. The cleaning schedule exists and is more or less followed, even if informally.

2 : 3S Audit Score of 80%. The areas (Work + in progress) are systematically cleaned at the end of each shift by the operators.

3 : Work areas are cleaned in "real time". Supply and replenishment activities are synchronized with production operations.

4 : The audit score (3S) is consistently above 90%. The audit requirements are continuously improving. A refresher course is conducted annually.
answers.question: Mastery of the first 3 "S"s
answers.action_plan: Sceheduled weekly audit in all areas

answers.description:
0 : There are no rules, and therefore no respect for them.

1 : Rules exist but are only respected when the hierarchy intervenes. The flow is not clearly identified.

2 :  The rules are known, accepted, and respected. They concern raw materials, work in progress, waste,

3 : Audits are conducted regularly. 5S audit scores are above 80%. Most areas are identified. The workflow is quite clear.

4 : Improvements are proposed to optimize the work environment; operators are the driving force.
answers.question: Mastery of the last two "S"s
answers.action_plan: Weekly audit schedued for all areas in the lab and reviewed in the tier meeting

answers.description:
0 : There are no information meetings within the teams, or else: team members are repeatedly absent or late. Meetings are held on an ad hoc basis; they are not routine.

1 :  Weekly information meetings are used to discuss major Unit topics. The ritual is not always respected: Location, duration, time, etc.

2 :  The weekly information meeting is a ritual in front of the notice board; it is used to discuss the performance of the past week and possible improvements.

3 :  The weekly meeting is held without any absences or lateness. The agenda is followed, and issues are not addressed during the meeting (appointments are scheduled). The format is strictly adhered to."12345". Representatives from support services may be invited occasionally.

4 : The meeting is prepared through interviews with the facilitator's direct supervisor and related production functions (planning, quality, methods, etc.). A structured site visit precedes the meeting to gather relevant observations. The resulting data is then analyzed.
answers.question: Weekly meeting
answers.action_plan: Detailed are actions are picked and reviewed in the weekly meeting

answers.description:
0 : Daily rituals are not formal. If rules exist, they are often not followed. The companions are left to their own devices at the start of their shift.

1 : Progress and key points (safety, quality, and maintenance) are recorded. Informal oral exchanges take place between direct supervisors and team members or colleagues. The area manager assigns everyone to their workstation. The established procedures have been described.

2 : The rituals take place. The agenda is generally followed. Absences and lateness are noted. The facilitators use a supporting document (paper) and record the main points on a daily chart.

3 : The discussion of the previous day's problems leads to corrective actions. The facilitator's direct supervisor sometimes attends the meeting.

4 : The routines—shift start, shift end, and shift handover—are perfectly followed in all areas. No delays, no time overruns, and adherence to the agenda. Corrective actions are implemented and monitored.
answers.question: Daily rituals of frontline management.
answers.action_plan: Reviewed on daily basis with all analyst in attendance together with the lab manager and supervisor. External stakeholders are also invited to attend when required

answers.description:
0 :  The board doesn't operate regularly. It's present on the ground, but not in a structured way and without a specific purpose.

1 : Management rotates regularly, but without any structured process. They use their observations immediately after each rotation, in an informal manner.

2 : The tours are ritualized and can be thematic. A formalized assessment tool for the standards to be monitored is in place. It includes quality, safety, 5S, and adherence to rules.

3 : The progress of the practices monitored during field visits is tracked over time. Field visits are known to and valued by operators as a way to gather feedback.

4 : Any deviation from accepted standards is addressed through an action plan. Middle management participates regularly in the review. Senior management participates occasionally.
answers.question: Structured field tour by the shift leader.
answers.action_plan: There is monthly site walkabout with the lab manager. Review and feedback is done on weekly basis during the weekly meetings

answers.description:
0 : No display or outdated display or notice boards exist and everyone is free to display the information they deem relevant.

1 :  Designated areas are provided for displaying zone information. A list of information/indicators to be displayed has been defined. The display is implemented and kept up to date.

2 : dedicated space is provided for displaying objectives. A list of information/indicators to be displayed has been defined. The display is implemented and kept up to date. The operators have a good understanding of the zone indicators.

3 :  The operators have a good understanding of the line indicators. The overall presentation of the display boards complies with the 5S principles.

4 :  The content of the table display is reviewed every two years (involving team members) to remove unnecessary information and add relevant information.
answers.question: Display
answers.action_plan: Action plans are reviewed on daily and weekly basis and closed out actions are deleted from the system

answers.description:
0 : There are no on-the-ground indicators. There is no data collection and no performance target.

1 :  Indicators exist for the zones and are tailored to the target audiences (relevant for the manager and understandable by the operators). They are updated regularly. All operators are aware of their contribution.

2 : Objectives exist for each zone; they are realistic and ambitious. Zone indicators are discussed during the corresponding rituals. Indicators also exist for the line; they are tailored to the audience and regularly updated.

3 :  Objectives exist for each line of work; they are realistic and ambitious. Line indicators are discussed during the corresponding rituals. Each deviation from the objective elicits a reaction from management (why?).

4 : Team members suggest new indicators and propose action plans to respond to them. Objectives are regularly met and raised.
answers.question: Terrain Indicators & Walking Target
answers.action_plan: KPIs are reviewed daily to meeting specifications. Actions are taken for out of spec parameters

answers.description:
0 : No self-control

1 : Self-monitoring is defined and implemented on the most critical production workstations. However, reliability is not verified.

2 : Self-inspection is in place at all workstations and the results are recorded. Operators have been trained and know the procedure to follow in case of a defect. Standards are posted at the workstation. Records are sometimes incomplete.

3 : Self-monitoring is monitored and audited by management; records are complete. A system for reporting defects detected at the customer's site exists and allows for monitoring effectiveness.

4 : Operators make proposals to improve quality. Standards are reviewed taking into account customer feedback.
answers.question: Self-control
answers.action_plan: Shift base pack dress assessment done and external audit is also done for products in the warehouse and the distributors warehouse

answers.description:
0 : No detection of non-conformities in production or no monitoring

1 :  Non-conforming product types are identified. Non-conforming products are detected, isolated, and processed using informal procedures. They are then reported.

2 : Formalized procedures exist for handling non-conformities. The types of non-conformities are recorded and classified (ABC diagram), the results are displayed, and the costs of the non-conformities are calculated.

3 : The main non-conformities are addressed through problem-solving groups, and action plans are implemented. Quality indices and customer feedback (PHN) are displayed and communicated to staff.

4 : 80% of non-conformities are addressed through problem-solving groups. A positive trend in quality assurance results and customer feedback has been observed following the actions taken.
answers.question: Mastering NCs in production
answers.action_plan: Procedure for non-standard procedure available and also procedure for addressing product non-comformities available

answers.description:
0 : The teams are not aware of the concept of OEE and are not conscious of the losses.

1 :  The OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) is measured systematically and the results are displayed.

2 : Improvement targets are set. The main losses are systematically identified (Pareto analysis). Actions to eliminate losses are carried out periodically.

3 : Improvement rituals take place every week. They are prepared, a report is written, and an action plan is developed. The action plan is reviewed weekly, posted, and communicated to the operators. Participants are not trained in problem-solving.

4 : Problem-solving tools are used systematically, and several members are trained in problem-solving. Operators and mechanics participate in these meetings. action_plan deadlines are met, results are improving, and objectives are achieved.
answers.question: Handling yield losses
answers.action_plan: Reviewis done on weekly basis

answers.description:
0 :  The teams are not aware of the concept of shrinkage and are not conscious of the losses

1 : The shrinkage is measured systematically and the results are displayed.

2 : Improvement targets are set. The main losses are systematically identified (Pareto analysis). Actions to eliminate losses are carried out periodically.

3 : Improvement rituals take place every week. They are prepared, a report is written, and an action plan is developed. The action plan is reviewed weekly, posted, and communicated to the operators. Participants are not trained in problem-solving.

4 : Problem-solving tools are used systematically, and several members are trained in problem-solving. Operators and mechanics participate in these meetings. action_plan deadlines are met, results are improving, and objectives are achieved.
answers.question: Treatment of shrinkages
answers.action_plan: Weekly review of RCPS density log in place

answers.description:
0 : Standard worksheet unknown.

1 : Standard worksheets exist, are designed by the various departments (methods, maintenance, etc.), but are rarely, if ever, used.

2 : The standard worksheet technique is well-known and frequently applied.

3 : The use of standard worksheets is widespread across all activities; operators are associated with their creation. Deviations from the standard worksheet are occasionally treated as problems to be solved.

4 : Standards are the foundation of continuous improvement. Deviations from the standard worksheet are systematically treated as problems to be solved. A history of the problems addressed is kept.
answers.question: Working with standards
answers.action_plan: Costant review of the standards and refresher training done for all analyst

answers.description:
0 : Approximate knowledge of series changeover times. Diverging opinions. Imprecise definition.

1 : Changeover times are systematically measured on all machines. The recordings are available.

2 : A pilot project on one machine was carried out. Staff were trained and participated. The standard is displayed.

3 :  Approximately 50% of the machines are operating under SMED. Implementation plan established.

4 :  All equipment is manufactured using SMED (Single Minute Exchange of Die). This process is repeated on at least one machine each year to continuously improve performance.
answers.question: SMED (CIP between brews/filtration cycles)
answers.action_plan: This done and tracked on daily basis,

answers.description:
0 : The operators do not perform the cleaning and lubrication themselves. Furthermore, the concept of autonomous maintenance is unknown.

3 : The necessary tools for carrying out the checks are available in the area and managed. Some technical improvements are being made to the equipment to facilitate self-maintenance.

4 :  Improvements are made to cleaning, lubrication and inspection standards every year
answers.question: Autonomous maintenance
answers.action_plan: Autonomous maintenance is scheduled and monitored in SAP

answers.description:

4 : The anomaly handling procedure is strictly followed: frequency, agenda, duration, etc. The effectiveness of the handling is reflected in the decrease in the failure rate. Some operators suggest improvements based on the anomalies they observe.
answers.question: Detection and reporting of anomalies
answers.action_plan: All anomalies are captured and recorded in the SIC for each area. There is also an escalation procedure to ensure issues are escalated on time

answers.description:

4 : Designated operators are able to perform adjustment or preventive maintenance operations based on standards. Improvements are made to preventive maintenance standards every year.
answers.question: Preventive maintenance
answers.action_plan: There is daily review of preventive maintenace schedule for the lab and validated by the lab supervisor

answers.description:

answers.question: Corrective Maintenance
answers.action_plan: Weekly review of maintenance schedule

Aside the safety KPIs being reviewed at Tier meetings on shift and daily basis. There's also an action in place to use the TV screens
 in packaging area to display communications on KPI's ,Safety incidents and creation of awareness.Daily walkabout is in place however  monthly structured safety walkabouts will need to be embedded as an improvement on the current practice.

3 : Frontline managers gain the team's buy-in and motivation. The team is able to define and implement common rules.

4 :  The team functions perfectly by applying the rules it has defined. Team cohesion is evident.
answers.question: Behavior and role of frontline management.
answers.action_plan: Trainings should be organized on emotional intelligence and active listening for team members as part of development plan.

answers.description:
0 : Versatility is not managed. Generally, each person sticks to their assigned role.

1 : The skills of the operators at their workstations are known but not formalized. And the use of the tool is limited to managing leave and absences.

2 : The tool (matrix P) exists, is formatted, and is displayed. The update frequency is determined and adhered to. The tool is used in the daily management of team work. Staff rotations are planned and followed.

3 : Management uses the tool to develop its teams by creating the annual training plan. The same principle is used to manage the positions of foreman and area manager.

4 : The team has taken ownership of the tool: it is constantly being updated. They provide feedback to management regarding training needs and suggestions for organization, etc. The training plan takes into account the needs expressed by staff and discussed with management.
answers.question: Versatility
answers.action_plan:
Organize cross training and upskilling trainings for team members 
Conduct  competency assessment to identify gaps and draw relevant training programs to close gaps identified.

answers.description:
0 : No PPE prescribed, no monitoring.

1 : PPE is prescribed but not worn or displayed. PPE exists, is kept in good condition, and is properly stored.

2 : PPE is prescribed and displayed, by workstation, made available and partially worn by employees (at least 80%). PPE is made available to visitors.

3 : PPE is worn by employees and visitors (at least 90%). It is managed by the team: sufficient quantities are available, and a replenishment method is defined.

4 : PPE is worn by all employees and visitors (100%). Requirements are reviewed regularly (at least once a year), in conjunction with risk assessments.
answers.question: Personal protective equipment
answers.action_plan: More safety trainings and awareness

answers.description:
0 : No safety signage. No specific communication on the subject.

1 : There is a display of the number of accidents resulting in lost time. The corresponding objective is defined. HSE audits are conducted at least once a year by the QHSE department. Safety is a priority topic during the shift handover and handover rituals.

2 : The HSE objectives are defined and understood by management. Staff feel that safety is a priority for management. Every lost-time accident is analyzed and an action plan is developed within 48 hours. The workshop manager participates in the annual HSE audits.

3 : First aid procedures are recorded and posted. Once a month, the workshop manager's structured safety walkthrough is dedicated to safety. On a daily basis, key safety points are checked through these structured walkthroughs.

4 : Near misses are reported and addressed. First aid incidents are analyzed twice a year, resulting in an action plan. A safety review, led by the line manager, takes place quarterly and covers all safety aspects of the line.
answers.question: Animation, Communication, Safety Display
answers.action_plan: Use animations to communicate safety risk

answers.description:
0 : Few defined and known rules. Little formal marking of fire extinguishers and fire hose reels. No first response team formed.

1 :  Critical workstations have safety instructions. Most fire extinguishers and fire hose reels are clearly marked.

2 : With very few exceptions, all workstations have safety instructions in addition to PPE guidelines. Fire extinguishers and fire hose reels are located and considered to be in sufficient quantity. A risk assessment has been carried out for the entire area.

3 : Safety instructions are posted and staff are trained (based on risk assessment). There are sufficient trained intervention and rescue teams for the area. Recycling is carried out regularly.

4 : Safety improvements are regularly implemented at workstations. Revised safety rules are posted, known, and followed by everyone. Staff are trained accordingly.
answers.question: Risk management

answers.description:
0 : There are no rules, and therefore no respect for them.

1 : Rules exist but are only respected when the hierarchy intervenes. The flow is not clearly identified.

2 :  The rules are known, accepted, and respected. They concern raw materials, work in progress, waste,

3 : Audits are conducted regularly. 5S audit scores are above 80%. Most areas are identified. The workflow is quite clear.

4 : Improvements are proposed to optimize the work environment; operators are the driving force.
answers.question: Mastery of the last two "S"s
answers.action_plan: Run project

answers.description:
0 : There are no information meetings within the teams, or else: team members are repeatedly absent or late. Meetings are held on an ad hoc basis; they are not routine.

1 :  Weekly information meetings are used to discuss major Unit topics. The ritual is not always respected: Location, duration, time, etc.

2 :  The weekly information meeting is a ritual in front of the notice board; it is used to discuss the performance of the past week and possible improvements.

3 :  The weekly meeting is held without any absences or lateness. The agenda is followed, and issues are not addressed during the meeting (appointments are scheduled). The format is strictly adhered to."12345". Representatives from support services may be invited occasionally.

4 : The meeting is prepared through interviews with the facilitator's direct supervisor and related production functions (planning, quality, methods, etc.). A structured site visit precedes the meeting to gather relevant observations. The resulting data is then analyzed.
answers.question: Weekly meeting
answers.action_plan: Weekly meetings happens but must be improved to involve the shop floor

answers.description:
0 : Daily rituals are not formal. If rules exist, they are often not followed. The companions are left to their own devices at the start of their shift.

1 : Progress and key points (safety, quality, and maintenance) are recorded. Informal oral exchanges take place between direct supervisors and team members or colleagues. The area manager assigns everyone to their workstation. The established procedures have been described.

2 : The rituals take place. The agenda is generally followed. Absences and lateness are noted. The facilitators use a supporting document (paper) and record the main points on a daily chart.

3 : The discussion of the previous day's problems leads to corrective actions. The facilitator's direct supervisor sometimes attends the meeting.

4 : The routines—shift start, shift end, and shift handover—are perfectly followed in all areas. No delays, no time overruns, and adherence to the agenda. Corrective actions are implemented and monitored.
answers.question: Daily rituals of frontline management.
answers.action_plan: Very vibrate meetings but can be improved

answers.description:
0 :  The board doesn't operate regularly. It's present on the ground, but not in a structured way and without a specific purpose.

1 : Management rotates regularly, but without any structured process. They use their observations immediately after each rotation, in an informal manner.

2 : The tours are ritualized and can be thematic. A formalized assessment tool for the standards to be monitored is in place. It includes quality, safety, 5S, and adherence to rules.

3 : The progress of the practices monitored during field visits is tracked over time. Field visits are known to and valued by operators as a way to gather feedback.

4 : Any deviation from accepted standards is addressed through an action plan. Middle management participates regularly in the review. Senior management participates occasionally.
answers.question: Structured field tour by the shift leader.
answers.action_plan: Very structured but can be improved with more accountability

answers.description:
0 : No display or outdated display or notice boards exist and everyone is free to display the information they deem relevant.

1 :  Designated areas are provided for displaying zone information. A list of information/indicators to be displayed has been defined. The display is implemented and kept up to date.

2 : dedicated space is provided for displaying objectives. A list of information/indicators to be displayed has been defined. The display is implemented and kept up to date. The operators have a good understanding of the zone indicators.

3 :  The operators have a good understanding of the line indicators. The overall presentation of the display boards complies with the 5S principles.

4 :  The content of the table display is reviewed every two years (involving team members) to remove unnecessary information and add relevant information.
answers.question: Display
answers.action_plan: Trainings on 5s

answers.description:
0 : There are no on-the-ground indicators. There is no data collection and no performance target.

1 :  Indicators exist for the zones and are tailored to the target audiences (relevant for the manager and understandable by the operators). They are updated regularly. All operators are aware of their contribution.

2 : Objectives exist for each zone; they are realistic and ambitious. Zone indicators are discussed during the corresponding rituals. Indicators also exist for the line; they are tailored to the audience and regularly updated.

3 :  Objectives exist for each line of work; they are realistic and ambitious. Line indicators are discussed during the corresponding rituals. Each deviation from the objective elicits a reaction from management (why?).

4 : Team members suggest new indicators and propose action plans to respond to them. Objectives are regularly met and raised.
answers.question: Terrain Indicators & Walking Target
answers.action_plan: Drive accountability

answers.description:
0 : No self-control

1 : Self-monitoring is defined and implemented on the most critical production workstations. However, reliability is not verified.

2 : Self-inspection is in place at all workstations and the results are recorded. Operators have been trained and know the procedure to follow in case of a defect. Standards are posted at the workstation. Records are sometimes incomplete.

3 : Self-monitoring is monitored and audited by management; records are complete. A system for reporting defects detected at the customer's site exists and allows for monitoring effectiveness.

4 : Operators make proposals to improve quality. Standards are reviewed taking into account customer feedback.
answers.question: Self-control
answers.action_plan: Drive accountability and trainings

answers.description:
0 : No detection of non-conformities in production or no monitoring

1 :  Non-conforming product types are identified. Non-conforming products are detected, isolated, and processed using informal procedures. They are then reported.

2 : Formalized procedures exist for handling non-conformities. The types of non-conformities are recorded and classified (ABC diagram), the results are displayed, and the costs of the non-conformities are calculated.

3 : The main non-conformities are addressed through problem-solving groups, and action plans are implemented. Quality indices and customer feedback (PHN) are displayed and communicated to staff.

4 : 80% of non-conformities are addressed through problem-solving groups. A positive trend in quality assurance results and customer feedback has been observed following the actions taken.
answers.question: Mastering NCs in production
answers.action_plan: Nonconformities are addressed through problem solving discussions but not visible to customer

answers.description:
0 : The teams are not aware of the concept of OEE and are not conscious of the losses.

1 :  The OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) is measured systematically and the results are displayed.

2 : Improvement targets are set. The main losses are systematically identified (Pareto analysis). Actions to eliminate losses are carried out periodically.

3 : Improvement rituals take place every week. They are prepared, a report is written, and an action plan is developed. The action plan is reviewed weekly, posted, and communicated to the operators. Participants are not trained in problem-solving.

answers.description:

answers.description:
